programme.title: Marooned with Ed Stafford 2
programme.desc: Former British Army captain and experienced traveler Ed Stafford continues to overcome new challenges.He became known to viewers as a steadfast man who is able to cope with any extreme situation.So far, among others:he underwent a test of survival for 60 days on one of the uninhabited islands of Fiji, where he had no supplies of food, clothing or necessary equipment.Moreover, he walked the trail leading along the Amazon River, which earned him a place in history and the Guinness Book of Records.In the next series of the program "Ed Stafford Beyond Civilization", the traveler once again sets off to the most hostile corners of the world, including the Gobi Desert in Mongolia and the crocodile-infested Guatemalan jungle.

programme.title: Marooned with Ed Stafford 2
programme.desc: Former British Army captain and experienced traveler Ed Stafford continues to overcome new challenges.He became known to viewers as a steadfast man who is able to cope with any extreme situation.So far, among others:he underwent a test of survival for 60 days on one of the uninhabited islands of Fiji, where he had no supplies of food, clothing or necessary equipment.Moreover, he walked the trail leading along the Amazon River, which earned him a place in history and the Guinness Book of Records.In the next series of the program "Ed Stafford Beyond Civilization", the traveler once again sets off to the most hostile corners of the world, including the Gobi Desert in Mongolia and the crocodile-infested Guatemalan jungle.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman travels around the United States to face the greatest culinary challenges.It covers the route from Boston to Seattle.He also goes to any other place where he can overeat.

programme.title: Island Life 7
programme.desc: The program presents the stories of people who wanted to live on an island, in a picturesque area, away from the hustle and bustle of the city. There are those who want a tropical climate and those who would like to live on islands in the Washington area and have a view of the mountains.Real estate agents will present properties in various price ranges to program participants.From those at an extremely affordable price to the most expensive and luxurious ones.Viewers will find out what the enthusiasts of island life will choose and whether their dreams will finally become a reality.

programme.title: Island Life 7
programme.desc: The program presents the stories of people who wanted to live on an island, in a picturesque area, away from the hustle and bustle of the city. There are those who want a tropical climate and those who would like to live on islands in the Washington area and have a view of the mountains.Real estate agents will present properties in various price ranges to program participants.From those at an extremely affordable price to the most expensive and luxurious ones.Viewers will find out what the enthusiasts of island life will choose and whether their dreams will finally become a reality.

programme.title: Island Life 7
programme.desc: The program presents the stories of people who wanted to live on an island, in a picturesque area, away from the hustle and bustle of the city. There are those who want a tropical climate and those who would like to live on islands in the Washington area and have a view of the mountains.Real estate agents will present properties in various price ranges to program participants.From those at an extremely affordable price to the most expensive and luxurious ones.Viewers will find out what the enthusiasts of island life will choose and whether their dreams will finally become a reality.

programme.title: Island Life 7
programme.desc: The program presents the stories of people who wanted to live on an island, in a picturesque area, away from the hustle and bustle of the city. There are those who want a tropical climate and those who would like to live on islands in the Washington area and have a view of the mountains.Real estate agents will present properties in various price ranges to program participants.From those at an extremely affordable price to the most expensive and luxurious ones.Viewers will find out what the enthusiasts of island life will choose and whether their dreams will finally become a reality.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman travels around the United States to face the greatest culinary challenges.It covers the route from Boston to Seattle.He also goes to any other place where he can overeat.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman travels around the United States to face the greatest culinary challenges.It covers the route from Boston to Seattle.He also goes to any other place where he can overeat.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman travels around the United States to face the greatest culinary challenges.It covers the route from Boston to Seattle.He also goes to any other place where he can overeat.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman travels around the United States to face the greatest culinary challenges.It covers the route from Boston to Seattle.He also goes to any other place where he can overeat.

programme.title: Taste the world with Pascal
programme.desc: Pascal Brodnicki likes to explore the world through his sense of taste.He loves to prepare various dishes at street stalls, in African villages or on the beach. However, during his culinary journeys it was not always easy for him.Sometimes elephants stole his ingredients.Of course, Pascal couldn't deny himself the pleasure of tasting local delicacies.In Delhi, he stopped for a long time at a paratha stand, in a Zanzibar village he ate rice with coconut, and in Namibia he tried a zebra steak."Taste the world with Pascal" is not only about cooking.The host visited an elephant orphanage in Sri Lanka, fed cheetahs in Africa and climbed a coconut tree.In Bombay he became a teacher and taught Polish to children, and on the coast of Zanzibar he hunted octopus.Everywhere his passion and positive energy attracted crowds of onlookers The first stop on Pascal's culinary journey is Sri Lanka.The cook visits the Pinawela center - an elephant orphanage.These huge mammals can eat up to 300 kg of food a day and they love carrots.Pascal will prepare a meal for these unusual gourmets.After a busy day, the chef decides to thank his newfound friends and invites them for dinner on the shores of the Indian Ocean.He chooses a quick recipe for chicken curry with the addition of cashew nuts, which are very popular on the island.

programme.title: Taste the world with Pascal
programme.desc: Pascal Brodnicki likes to explore the world through his sense of taste.He loves to prepare various dishes at street stalls, in African villages or on the beach. However, during his culinary journeys it was not always easy for him.Sometimes elephants stole his ingredients.Of course, Pascal couldn't deny himself the pleasure of tasting local delicacies.In Delhi, he stopped for a long time at a paratha stand, in a Zanzibar village he ate rice with coconut, and in Namibia he tried a zebra steak."Taste the world with Pascal" is not only about cooking.The host visited an elephant orphanage in Sri Lanka, fed cheetahs in Africa and climbed a coconut tree.In Bombay he became a teacher and taught Polish to children, and on the coast of Zanzibar he hunted octopus.Everywhere his passion and positive energy attracted crowds of onlookers Pascal continues to explore the flavors of Sri Lanka - a country that is a leader in tea production.The cook decides to learn the secrets of working on the plantation.The appearance of an almost two-meter tall man will cause quite a sensation among the women working there.Pascal will also visit Negombo.The city on the west coast of the island is known mainly for its old market where you can buy various types of fish - from mackerel, through shark, to "flying fish".All fresh, straight from the sea.While exploring the coast, Pascal meets local fishermen.He offers them help, hoping that he will learn the secrets of preparing fish.However, it quickly turns out that he will have to prove himself as a trade fair seller.

programme.title: Taste the world with Pascal
programme.desc: Pascal Brodnicki likes to explore the world through his sense of taste.He loves to prepare various dishes at street stalls, in African villages or on the beach. However, during his culinary journeys it was not always easy for him.Sometimes elephants stole his ingredients.Of course, Pascal couldn't deny himself the pleasure of tasting local delicacies.In Delhi, he stopped for a long time at a paratha stand, in a Zanzibar village he ate rice with coconut, and in Namibia he tried a zebra steak."Taste the world with Pascal" is not only about cooking.The host visited an elephant orphanage in Sri Lanka, fed cheetahs in Africa and climbed a coconut tree.In Bombay he became a teacher and taught Polish to children, and on the coast of Zanzibar he hunted octopus.Everywhere his passion and positive energy attracted crowds of onlookers This time Pascal went to Namibia, famous for its cow breeding and delicious beef.The chef grills his favorite steaks here and, as usual, he will do it in an unusual place.This time on the farm where they are kept.tame cheetahs.After meeting the big cats, Pascal sets off north of the country, to Opuwo, where he goes to a supermarket.Here he meets half-naked women from the Himba tribe who are shopping.He also visits the market where he buys a gift for the Herrero tribe - a goat.His gesture wins great acclaim and Pascal is welcomed into the village with an amazing dance.

programme.title: Taste the world with Pascal
programme.desc: Pascal Brodnicki likes to explore the world through his sense of taste.He loves to prepare various dishes at street stalls, in African villages or on the beach. However, during his culinary journeys it was not always easy for him.Sometimes elephants stole his ingredients.Of course, Pascal couldn't deny himself the pleasure of tasting local delicacies.In Delhi, he stopped for a long time at a paratha stand, in a Zanzibar village he ate rice with coconut, and in Namibia he tried a zebra steak."Taste the world with Pascal" is not only about cooking.The host visited an elephant orphanage in Sri Lanka, fed cheetahs in Africa and climbed a coconut tree.In Bombay he became a teacher and taught Polish to children, and on the coast of Zanzibar he hunted octopus.Everywhere his passion and positive energy attracted crowds of onlookers The next stop on the "Taste the World with Pascal" map is India.Pascal will visit Delhi, where he will taste parathas, traditional wheat pancakes, served with green peas, carrots, bananas or cheese at street stalls.At one of the stalls, Pascal will serve passers-by chicken "Indian style" according to his own recipe.The leader will also visit temples where volunteers prepare free meals for pilgrims.In such kitchens, people cook for up to a thousand people.The cook had never cooked on such a scale.Later, Pascal will go to Bombay, where he will look for the perfect fish for his recipe at the fish market.And then he will prepare it in curry sauce, with peppers and coconut milk.A saleswoman at the local market lends him the kitchen.

programme.title: Indonesia's Best Ever Eats
programme.desc: A culinary journey to the largest island country in the world.Gourmets will discover the best dishes from five regions of Indonesia, including the area around Lake Toba in Sumatra.

programme.title: China according to Jarek Nowak
programme.desc: Jarek Nowak, anthropologist and traveler, goes on an expedition through China to rediscover one of the oldest civilizations in the world.The host will take viewers on a walk through the alleys of the Great Wall of China, tasting street food dishes and trekking through the Leaping Dragon Gorge.There will also be interesting attractions, surprising customs and unusual dishes.The traveler will advise you on how to get around, what to do in your free time and how to experience a once-in-a-lifetime adventure.The program is an alternative guide that contains a lot of interesting facts and practical tips for everyone.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman travels around the United States to face the greatest culinary challenges.It covers the route from Boston to Seattle.He also goes to any other place where he can overeat.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern visits various cities around the world.In each of them, he presents dishes typical of a given place.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern visits various cities around the world.In each of them, he presents dishes typical of a given place.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern visits various cities around the world.In each of them, he presents dishes typical of a given place.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern visits various cities around the world.In each of them, he presents dishes typical of a given place.

programme.title: World weddings
programme.desc: Wedding customs can vary greatly from country to country.In Sweden, it is unacceptable for any woman to wear a red dress.However, in China there is another interesting custom - a few days before the wedding, the bride must cry in the presence of other women from the family.Anna Szlęzak will go to a Peruvian village in the Andes to observe the local wedding traditions.Local couples live together on a trial basis before officially getting married.This period may last even several years.If the relationship turns out to be unsuccessful, they break up unless they have children.Otherwise, they are obliged to get married.The entire community, without exception, is involved in organizing the celebration.The host will not only learn about the superstitions and customs prevailing here, but will also keep the bride company just before the wedding.She will then attend an Indian wedding reception.

programme.title: World weddings
programme.desc: Wedding customs can vary greatly from country to country.In Sweden, it is unacceptable for any woman to wear a red dress.However, in China there is another interesting custom - a few days before the wedding, the bride must cry in the presence of other women from the family.Anna Szlęzak will go to a Peruvian village in the Andes to observe the local wedding traditions.Local couples live together on a trial basis before officially getting married.This period may last even several years.If the relationship turns out to be unsuccessful, they break up unless they have children.Otherwise, they are obliged to get married.The entire community, without exception, is involved in organizing the celebration.The host will not only learn about the superstitions and customs prevailing here, but will also keep the bride company just before the wedding.She will then attend an Indian wedding reception.

programme.title: Aloha, new home
programme.desc: Spouses Kamohai and Tristyn Kalama are native Hawaiians whose passion and mission is to renovate abandoned houses on the island of Oahu.Contrary to appearances, it is not just pleasant work in beautiful natural surroundings.Many of these properties are in a deplorable condition due to moisture, wind and sun.Renovations are additionally complicated by difficult terrain and changeable weather.However, the reward for designers is the end result, which is worth every moment spent renovating these amazing buildings.

programme.title: Aloha, new home
programme.desc: Spouses Kamohai and Tristyn Kalama are native Hawaiians whose passion and mission is to renovate abandoned houses on the island of Oahu.Contrary to appearances, it is not just pleasant work in beautiful natural surroundings.Many of these properties are in a deplorable condition due to moisture, wind and sun.Renovations are additionally complicated by difficult terrain and changeable weather.However, the reward for designers is the end result, which is worth every moment spent renovating these amazing buildings.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid 5
programme.desc: Two unknown people, a woman and a man, are left in a lonely place with difficult climatic conditions.They must spend 21 days there without food, water or clothing.They can only take one item with them.The operators show how daredevils, relying on their own company, learn to cooperate and make key decisions together.Viewers will see that appropriate knowledge and skills can be very useful in extreme moments and even determine survival.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid 5
programme.desc: Two unknown people, a woman and a man, are left in a lonely place with difficult climatic conditions.They must spend 21 days there without food, water or clothing.They can only take one item with them.The operators show how daredevils, relying on their own company, learn to cooperate and make key decisions together.Viewers will see that appropriate knowledge and skills can be very useful in extreme moments and even determine survival.

programme.title: Woman at the end of the world
programme.desc: Reports showing the stories of women living in Africa, Asia and America.The heroines have different professions and follow different religions.Presenting their fate is only a pretext to show the exoticism, traditions and culture of the countries in which they live.Martyna Wojciechowska, who kept a diary from these extraordinary journeys, experienced their joys and sorrows together with her interlocutors and participated in their everyday activities.This allowed her to understand contrasts and learn about different approaches to femininity Martyna Wojciechowska visits women who are sappers in the women's unit in Cambodia.The journalist tries to find out why they chose such a risky job.One of the heroines of the reportage claims that she decided to take up this job for financial reasons.With the money he earns, he can support his family.So Tonh, 40, who is the group's commander, sees her son and daughter several days a year.However, he is happy that he can pay for their education.Every day he puts his life at risk to provide children with a decent living and a better future.

programme.title: Woman at the end of the world
programme.desc: Reports showing the stories of women living in Africa, Asia and America.The heroines have different professions and follow different religions.Presenting their fate is only a pretext to show the exoticism, traditions and culture of the countries in which they live.Martyna Wojciechowska, who kept a diary from these extraordinary journeys, experienced their joys and sorrows together with her interlocutors and participated in their everyday activities.This allowed her to understand contrasts and learn about different approaches to femininity Martyna Wojciechowska's guide in Kenya is Daphne Sheldrick, who has devoted fifty years of her life to fighting for the protection of animal rights.Together with her husband, she founded an orphanage for baby elephants - victims of brutal poachers.The journalist finds out what it's like to care for a baby elephant that requires a lot of attention, has a huge appetite and loves to play.The heroine of this episode has been working for fourteen years to create a milk recipe suitable for her children.Today he knows everything about them and is a legend in Kenya.

programme.title: Woman at the end of the world
programme.desc: Reports showing the stories of women living in Africa, Asia and America.The heroines have different professions and follow different religions.Presenting their fate is only a pretext to show the exoticism, traditions and culture of the countries in which they live.Martyna Wojciechowska, who kept a diary from these extraordinary journeys, experienced their joys and sorrows together with her interlocutors and participated in their everyday activities.This allowed her to understand contrasts and learn about different approaches to femininity.

programme.title: Indonesia's Best Ever Eats
programme.desc: A culinary journey to the largest island country in the world.Gourmets will discover the best dishes from five regions of Indonesia, including the area around Lake Toba in Sumatra.

programme.title: China according to Jarek Nowak
programme.desc: Jarek Nowak, anthropologist and traveler, goes on an expedition through China to rediscover one of the oldest civilizations in the world.The host will take viewers on a walk through the alleys of the Great Wall of China, tasting street food dishes and trekking through the Leaping Dragon Gorge.There will also be interesting attractions, surprising customs and unusual dishes.The traveler will advise you on how to get around, what to do in your free time and how to experience a once-in-a-lifetime adventure.The program is an alternative guide that contains a lot of interesting facts and practical tips for everyone.

programme.title: World weddings
programme.desc: Wedding customs can vary greatly from country to country.In Sweden, it is unacceptable for any woman to wear a red dress.However, in China there is another interesting custom - a few days before the wedding, the bride must cry in the presence of other women from the family.Anna Szlęzak will go to a Peruvian village in the Andes to observe the local wedding traditions.Local couples live together on a trial basis before officially getting married.This period may last even several years.If the relationship turns out to be unsuccessful, they break up unless they have children.Otherwise, they are obliged to get married.The entire community, without exception, is involved in organizing the celebration.The host will not only learn about the superstitions and customs prevailing here, but will also keep the bride company just before the wedding.She will then attend an Indian wedding reception.

programme.title: World weddings
programme.desc: Wedding customs can vary greatly from country to country.In Sweden, it is unacceptable for any woman to wear a red dress.However, in China there is another interesting custom - a few days before the wedding, the bride must cry in the presence of other women from the family.Anna Szlęzak will go to a Peruvian village in the Andes to observe the local wedding traditions.Local couples live together on a trial basis before officially getting married.This period may last even several years.If the relationship turns out to be unsuccessful, they break up unless they have children.Otherwise, they are obliged to get married.The entire community, without exception, is involved in organizing the celebration.The host will not only learn about the superstitions and customs prevailing here, but will also keep the bride company just before the wedding.She will then attend an Indian wedding reception.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman travels around the United States to face the greatest culinary challenges.It covers the route from Boston to Seattle.He also goes to any other place where he can overeat.

programme.title: Caribbean Life 19
programme.desc: The fate of families who decided to move to the Caribbean.They need to find a suitable home.In each episode, the characters will visit several properties to fulfill their dream of living in paradise.

programme.title: Caribbean Life 19
programme.desc: The fate of families who decided to move to the Caribbean.They need to find a suitable home.In each episode, the characters will visit several properties to fulfill their dream of living in paradise.

programme.title: Caribbean Life 19
programme.desc: The fate of families who decided to move to the Caribbean.They need to find a suitable home.In each episode, the characters will visit several properties to fulfill their dream of living in paradise.

programme.title: Caribbean Life 19
programme.desc: The fate of families who decided to move to the Caribbean.They need to find a suitable home.In each episode, the characters will visit several properties to fulfill their dream of living in paradise.

programme.title: The Extraordinary States of Procopius
programme.desc: Marcin Prokop sets off overseas once again.According to the stereotype, the United States is a place of endless possibilities, where everyone can fulfill their dreams.In the new season of the program, the host will meet people who have found the recipe for success in life.In this way, he wants to find out whether there is a set of universal rules that guarantee happiness.He will visit, among others, the Skid Row district in Los Angeles, the NASA headquarters in Pasadena, the desert in Utah and a gold mine in Colorado.In each episode, viewers will discover new stories and places In this episode, Marcin Prokop sets off on a journey to Silicon Valley.But first, he will visit Sausalito, a city called the Venice of America.In this unique place, houses are built on the water, and residents are separated from the bustling San Francisco by a trip over the Golden Gate Bridge.However, in the heart of Silicon Valley there is Stanford University.This prestigious university counts among its graduates, among others, Elon Musk.This is where Han will tell the host about how to create your own company in the center of American technological thought.Marcin Prokop will also meet Jakub Pachocki.This will be a great opportunity to ask one of the creators of ChatGPT a few questions generated by this tool.

programme.title: The Extraordinary States of Procopius
programme.desc: Marcin Prokop sets off overseas once again.According to the stereotype, the United States is a place of endless possibilities, where everyone can fulfill their dreams.In the new season of the program, the host will meet people who have found the recipe for success in life.In this way, he wants to find out whether there is a set of universal rules that guarantee happiness.He will visit, among others, the Skid Row district in Los Angeles, the NASA headquarters in Pasadena, the desert in Utah and a gold mine in Colorado.In each episode, viewers will discover new stories and places Opened in 1894, Huntsville Prison is an institution where over 580 people lost their lives.Executions of convicts were carried out using an electric chair, which can now be seen as a museum exhibit.David Stacks, a former prison guard, will talk about working there and the emotions that accompany meetings with the most dangerous criminals in America.Jimi Willet, former prison warden and execution supervisor, will reveal whether he ever managed to get used to death.Marcin will also ask him about the impact his job has on his family life.

programme.title: COOL tourists
programme.desc: Rapper Vienio and chef Filip Głodek will go on a trip around European cities.They will not follow popular tourist trails, but will trample their own paths.

programme.title: COOL tourists
programme.desc: Rapper Vienio and chef Filip Głodek will go on a trip around European cities.They will not follow popular tourist trails, but will trample their own paths.

programme.title: Woman at the end of the world
programme.desc: The program returns to the air after a break.Over the last few months, Martyna Wojciechowska and her team have traveled all over the world to look for inspiring women who will talk about what is currently most important.For some topics, the host had to wait many years, but for the good stuff, it's definitely worth it.The latest season will bring meetings with the oldest and youngest heroines in the history of the show.Viewers will see episodes shot in eight countries and on five continents.However, it is not about records or the number of kilometers traveled.The most important thing is meeting another person, time spent together, intimate conversation, and the truth about the world.

programme.title: Woman at the end of the world
programme.desc: Martyna Wojciechowska has been wandering the world for many years and looking for interesting people who share their unique stories with her.The traveler not only talks about their everyday lives, but also raises important social issues.In the tenth season of the program he will visit, among others:Spitsbergen - the largest island of the Svalbard archipelago, where it is night for a quarter of the year.He will also reach out to the Wodaabe community living in Chad, and in El Salvador he will address the difficult issue of restrictive anti-abortion regulations.

programme.title: Woman at the end of the world
programme.desc: Martyna Wojciechowska has been wandering the world for many years and looking for interesting people who share their unique stories with her.The traveler not only talks about their everyday lives, but also raises important social issues.In the tenth season of the program he will visit, among others:Spitsbergen - the largest island of the Svalbard archipelago, where it is night for a quarter of the year.He will also reach out to the Wodaabe community living in Chad, and in El Salvador he will tackle the difficult issue of restrictive anti-abortion regulations. For years, the Iban people living in Borneo were considered one of the most terrifying communities in the world.Its members carried out ritual hunts for people.They believed that a warrior who shed blood gained strength and glory.Nowadays, this tradition is no longer cultivated, but its traces still remain in Iban homes.They never got rid of the trophies they won years ago.Martyna Wojciechowska talks to a descendant of bounty hunters.He also takes part in the skull taming ritual.

programme.title: Woman at the end of the world
programme.desc: Martyna Wojciechowska has been wandering the world for many years and looking for interesting people who share their unique stories with her.The traveler not only talks about their everyday lives, but also raises important social issues.In the tenth season of the program he will visit, among others:Spitsbergen - the largest island of the Svalbard archipelago, where it is night for a quarter of the year.He will also reach out to the Wodaabe community living in Chad, and in El Salvador he will tackle the difficult issue of restrictive anti-abortion regulations. Martyna Wojciechowska talks to Morena Herrera, who fights for women's rights in El Salvador.The country ranks first in terms of the number of murders of women.About 500 such cases are reported annually, with a population of less than 3 million.More than half of the victims of violence do not seek help and do not reveal the truth even to their closest family members.Moreover, El Salvador has one of the most restrictive anti-abortion laws in the world.Termination of pregnancy is always illegal.If a woman loses a child in unclear circumstances, she immediately becomes a suspect.The penalty for her ranges from 2 to 8 years, and if found guilty, she may be imprisoned for 50 years.A doctor can be sentenced to 6 to 12 years for performing an abortion.Anyone who knew about the situation but did not inform the authorities is also complicit.

programme.title: By truck across the United States
programme.desc: Dawid Andres will once again take advantage of the opportunity to combine delivering goods with exploring American nature, architecture and culture.With him, viewers will visit, among others:Atlanta and check out the Daytona track.

programme.title: By truck across the United States
programme.desc: Dawid Andres will once again take advantage of the opportunity to combine delivering goods with exploring American nature, architecture and culture.With him, viewers will visit, among others:Atlanta and check out the Daytona track.

programme.title: By truck across the United States
programme.desc: Dawid Andres will once again take advantage of the opportunity to combine delivering goods with exploring American nature, architecture and culture.With him, viewers will visit, among others:Atlanta and check out the Daytona track.

programme.title: Taste the world with Pascal
programme.desc: Pascal Brodnicki likes to explore the world through his sense of taste.He loves to prepare various dishes at street stalls, in African villages or on the beach. However, during his culinary journeys it was not always easy for him.Sometimes elephants stole his ingredients.Of course, Pascal couldn't deny himself the pleasure of tasting local delicacies.In Delhi, he stopped for a long time at a paratha stand, in a Zanzibar village he ate rice with coconut, and in Namibia he tried a zebra steak."Taste the world with Pascal" is not only about cooking.The host visited an elephant orphanage in Sri Lanka, fed cheetahs in Africa and climbed a coconut tree.In Bombay he became a teacher and taught Polish to children, and on the coast of Zanzibar he hunted octopus.Everywhere his passion and positive energy attracted crowds of onlookers The first stop on Pascal's culinary journey is Sri Lanka.The cook visits the Pinawela center - an elephant orphanage.These huge mammals can eat up to 300 kg of food a day and they love carrots.Pascal will prepare a meal for these unusual gourmets.After a busy day, the chef decides to thank his newfound friends and invites them for dinner on the shores of the Indian Ocean.He chooses a quick recipe for chicken curry with the addition of cashew nuts, which are very popular on the island.

programme.title: Taste the world with Pascal
programme.desc: Pascal Brodnicki likes to explore the world through his sense of taste.He loves to prepare various dishes at street stalls, in African villages or on the beach. However, during his culinary journeys it was not always easy for him.Sometimes elephants stole his ingredients.Of course, Pascal couldn't deny himself the pleasure of tasting local delicacies.In Delhi, he stopped for a long time at a paratha stand, in a Zanzibar village he ate rice with coconut, and in Namibia he tried a zebra steak."Taste the world with Pascal" is not only about cooking.The host visited an elephant orphanage in Sri Lanka, fed cheetahs in Africa and climbed a coconut tree.In Bombay he became a teacher and taught Polish to children, and on the coast of Zanzibar he hunted octopus.Everywhere his passion and positive energy attracted crowds of onlookers Pascal continues to explore the flavors of Sri Lanka - a country that is a leader in tea production.The cook decides to learn the secrets of working on the plantation.The appearance of an almost two-meter tall man will cause quite a sensation among the women working there.Pascal will also visit Negombo.The city on the west coast of the island is known mainly for its old market where you can buy various types of fish - from mackerel, through shark, to "flying fish".All fresh, straight from the sea.While exploring the coast, Pascal meets local fishermen.He offers them help, hoping that he will learn the secrets of preparing fish.However, it quickly turns out that he will have to prove himself as a trade fair seller.

programme.title: Taste the world with Pascal
programme.desc: Pascal Brodnicki likes to explore the world through his sense of taste.He loves to prepare various dishes at street stalls, in African villages or on the beach. However, during his culinary journeys it was not always easy for him.Sometimes elephants stole his ingredients.Of course, Pascal couldn't deny himself the pleasure of tasting local delicacies.In Delhi, he stopped for a long time at a paratha stand, in a Zanzibar village he ate rice with coconut, and in Namibia he tried a zebra steak."Taste the world with Pascal" is not only about cooking.The host visited an elephant orphanage in Sri Lanka, fed cheetahs in Africa and climbed a coconut tree.In Bombay he became a teacher and taught Polish to children, and on the coast of Zanzibar he hunted octopus.Everywhere his passion and positive energy attracted crowds of onlookers This time Pascal went to Namibia, famous for its cow breeding and delicious beef.The chef grills his favorite steaks here and, as usual, he will do it in an unusual place.This time on the farm where they are kept.tame cheetahs.After meeting the big cats, Pascal sets off north of the country, to Opuwo, where he goes to a supermarket.Here he meets half-naked women from the Himba tribe who are shopping.He also visits the market where he buys a gift for the Herrero tribe - a goat.His gesture wins great acclaim and Pascal is welcomed into the village with an amazing dance.

programme.title: Taste the world with Pascal
programme.desc: Pascal Brodnicki likes to explore the world through his sense of taste.He loves to prepare various dishes at street stalls, in African villages or on the beach. However, during his culinary journeys it was not always easy for him.Sometimes elephants stole his ingredients.Of course, Pascal couldn't deny himself the pleasure of tasting local delicacies.In Delhi, he stopped for a long time at a paratha stand, in a Zanzibar village he ate rice with coconut, and in Namibia he tried a zebra steak."Taste the world with Pascal" is not only about cooking.The host visited an elephant orphanage in Sri Lanka, fed cheetahs in Africa and climbed a coconut tree.In Bombay he became a teacher and taught Polish to children, and on the coast of Zanzibar he hunted octopus.Everywhere his passion and positive energy attracted crowds of onlookers The next stop on the "Taste the World with Pascal" map is India.Pascal will visit Delhi, where he will taste parathas, traditional wheat pancakes, served with green peas, carrots, bananas or cheese at street stalls.At one of the stalls, Pascal will serve passers-by chicken "Indian style" according to his own recipe.The leader will also visit temples where volunteers prepare free meals for pilgrims.In such kitchens, people cook for up to a thousand people.The cook had never cooked on such a scale.Later, Pascal will go to Bombay, where he will look for the perfect fish for his recipe at the fish market.And then he will prepare it in curry sauce, with peppers and coconut milk.A saleswoman at the local market lends him the kitchen.

programme.title: Shocking dishes
programme.desc: Jarek Nowak is looking for shocking dishes in Peru.He visits Iquitos - a city located on the Amazon River, to which no land route leads.Looking for extreme taste sensations, he reaches the market.There you can buy products straight from the jungle, such as turtles, crocodiles, fish and worms.Traders also sell magical herbs and unusual potions that can cure various ailments.Later, the host pays a visit to a tribe that eats monkeys, turtles, eggs, intestines with their contents, and spicy Karachama fish soup.

programme.title: Shocking dishes
programme.desc: Jarek Nowak goes to Singapore, a city that provides tourists with many delights and surprises.He will look for the strangest dishes and the most interesting delicacies of Asian cuisine.The tasting will start with bird's nest soup, the main ingredient of which is dried swallow saliva, which has extremely health-promoting properties.The locals consider it a real delicacy, and you have to pay dearly for a portion of the soup.Then Jarek will try sheep brain and shark head, before snacking on durian dessert, the smelliest fruit in the world.

programme.title: Indonesia's Best Ever Eats
programme.desc: A culinary journey to the largest island country in the world.Gourmets will discover the best dishes from five regions of Indonesia, including the area around Lake Toba in Sumatra.

programme.title: China according to Jarek Nowak
programme.desc: Jarek Nowak, anthropologist and traveler, goes on an expedition through China to rediscover one of the oldest civilizations in the world.The host will take viewers on a walk through the alleys of the Great Wall of China, tasting street food dishes and trekking through the Leaping Dragon Gorge.There will also be interesting attractions, surprising customs and unusual dishes.The traveler will advise you on how to get around, what to do in your free time and how to experience a once-in-a-lifetime adventure.The program is an alternative guide that contains a lot of interesting facts and practical tips for everyone.
